epic.status: PROPOSED — bdo's chat pitch 2026-06-10 (the 'onton benchmark'), mapped in discussion and named by him. The arc and the rung order are his to amend or stamp; 'Pivot' is his mint (the vault's word for the hidden center, Seam at (0,0,0)).
epic.arc: Turn the glyph cube from a described geometry into a measuring instrument: a benchmark suite that asks whether deliberately-hidden structure, encoded as a placement of meaning on the cube, is recoverable by natural model inference — and how deep that recovery survives recursion. Every rung grades against the incidence laws the cube already verifies, so the verdict is a number on a calibrated scale, not a vibe.
epic.context: Lineage is bdo's informal result: two models each hide a concept in a 20-word set around a shared theme; a third *naive* model recovers the hidden concept cold and even detects it is a test, never told to look. Pilish is the deterministic cousin — a hidden channel (word length -> pi digit) provably recoverable. Pivot generalizes both to a typed 3-D lattice: the address (fixed {-,0,+}^3 cell, polysheaf lettering) and the occupant (the word placed there, cube-alphabet) already exist in glyphs/viewer.html; the S-frame (language/s-frame-placements.json, 27 words, PROPOSED/MODEL-GUESSED) is a worked occupant population. The cube is self-checking (closure = 3^dim, star = 2^codim, Sigma = 125) — that determinism is what lets recovery be graded without a model-in-the-loop.
epic.value: A calibrated answer to 'is the hidden state we encode retrievable, and how much structure does the encoding actually carry' — random-floor <-> cube-placement <-> obvious-ceiling, with convergence-across-independent-inferences as the meaning metric where no gold label exists. The instrument is reusable for any onton encoding, not just the ontabet letters; its cube-vs-random verdict is the same one epic.ontabet-speaks needs (wave-3.measures) to gate A-Z spend.
epic.horizon: All five stages real on the same gradeable core: a cold (uncontaminated) recoverer plays each rung, the harness grades deterministically against the laws, results land as signed records, and the suite reports where each encoding sits on the calibrated scale and at what recursion depth blind recovery dies.
epic.spine: The harness never calls a model (no-network, stdlib, reuses knoll.py's cube). Inference is done by cold agents/sessions and lands as records. A session that has read the placements is contaminated as a scorer (epic.ontabet-speaks, wave-3.measures); the harness records recoverer provenance and refuses a contaminated grade.

pieces.glue: done-line 0031: structured placement recovery — cold agent gets occupants + bare container, hides the mapping, assigns coords; graded on cell-kind match + incidence/census laws across three populations (random floor, S-frame test, surface-encoded ceiling). One calibrated number. The frame the rest bolt onto.
recreation: hold out the center (Seam, (0,0,0)); the recoverer names the pivot from the 26-glyph shell. Generalizes to any held-out cell; exact-cell + laws grade it.

the difficulty dial: recover the placement from minimal cues — an axis orientation (collapses the symmetry, makes position-level well-posed), one facet, or nothing. Accuracy vs cues-given is the curve.

mixed state -> nominal state: given a scrambled occupant->address assignment, recover the solved one; distance-to-solved grades it. The address=occupant identity holds only at solve — this rung is where it is earned.

the naivety axis at full strength and the direct reproduction of bdo's pi/XYZ result: hand a cold model the word-set with NO container, NO axes; does it detect the structure and recover it? Needs a judge or a structured-elicitation grader — its own increment.
re-run the suite one level down (each cell is itself a cube; the viewer already does frame-descent); report the depth at which recovery falls to the random floor.
